title: Configuration
description: Learn to configure Playwright for different testing scenarios

questions.question: What is the default configuration file name for Playwright?
questions.explanation: playwright.config.ts (or .js) is the default configuration file for Playwright projects.

questions.question: You can configure different browser projects in playwright.config.ts.
questions.explanation: The projects configuration allows you to define different browser and device configurations.

questions.question: Complete the config to set base URL:
questions.explanation: The baseURL option sets the default URL for page.goto() calls with relative paths.

questions.question: Which configuration option sets the default timeout for actions?
questions.explanation: actionTimeout sets the maximum time for each action like click, fill, etc.

questions.question: Complete the config to run tests in parallel:
questions.explanation: The workers option controls the number of parallel worker processes for test execution.

questions.question: You can configure different viewport sizes for different test projects.
questions.explanation: Each project can have its own viewport configuration for testing different screen sizes.

questions.question: Where should the testDir option be configured?
options: At the root level of config
questions.explanation: testDir is a root-level configuration option that specifies where test files are located.

questions.question: What's wrong with this configuration?
questions.explanation: Should use defineConfig() for type safety, and browsers are configured in projects, not use.browser.

questions.question: Which reporter generates an HTML report?
questions.explanation: HTML reporter can be configured as a string 'html' or in array format [['html', options]].

questions.question: Complete the config to set screenshot on failure:
questions.explanation: screenshot: 'only-on-failure' captures screenshots automatically when tests fail.
